meta.title: NATO Public Forum: NATO’s Policy toward Russia
meta.description:
Russia represents a real and potentially existential threat to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization’s members and partners. This is why NATO’s 2022 Strategic Concept rightly stated that “the Russian Federation is the most significant and direct threat to Allies’ security and to peace and stability in the Euro-Atlantic area.” The outcome of the war in Ukraine will determine the geopolitical conditions across the transatlantic community for the foreseeable future. Therefore, what is the alliance doing to deal with the Russian threat as outlined in the recent Strategic Concept? What lessons from Ukraine are being learned about warfare in the twenty-first century? What steps will the alliance need to take in the future to maintain a warfighting edge over the Russian Federation? 

A Hudson panel at the 2024 NATO Public Forum discussed these topics and more with four distinguished defense ministers.
